title: LLM Decoding Mathematics
learning_outcomes: Compare Greedy and Beam Search decoding parameters.
Formulate Temperature-scaled softmax and Top-K/Top-P sampling equations.

content_sections.title: Greedy Search and Beam Search
content_sections.content:
LLMs output logits representing predictions over a vocabulary. To generate text, we decode these logits:

*   **Greedy Search:** Selects the token with the highest probability at each step. It is computationally cheap but often leads to repetitive or suboptimal sequences.
*   **Beam Search:** Keeps track of the top $B$ candidate sequences (beams) at each step, evaluating their cumulative probabilities. While computationally expensive, it yields better global optimization.

content_sections.title: Temperature Scaling, Top-K, and Top-P
content_sections.content:
To introduce diversity, we sample from the probability distribution. Logits are scaled using a temperature parameter $T > 0$:

$$p_i = \frac{\exp(z_i / T)}{\sum_j \exp(z_j / T)}$$

*   Low temperature ($T \to 0$) concentrates probability mass on the top token, approaching greedy search.
*   High temperature ($T > 1$) flattens the distribution, increasing diversity but raising the risk of incoherent outputs.

To restrict sampling to safe tokens, we apply filtering:
1.  **Top-K:** Selects only the $K$ highest-probability tokens.
2.  **Top-P (Nucleus Sampling):** Dynamically filters candidates by selecting the smallest subset of tokens whose cumulative probability exceeds threshold $P$:

$$\sum_{i \in \mathcal{V}_{Top-P}} P(w_i \mid w_{<t}) \ge P$$
